titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the Elysium Press Records
titlestmt.author: Monika Lehman

publicationstmt.publisher: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

maintenanceagency.agencyname: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

descriptivenote.p: Finding aid description and metadata in Archives at Yale are licensed under a CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. This means that anyone may copy, modify, and distribute this descriptive metadata without restriction or permission. The CC0 license is explained in full on the Creative Commons website: CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. More information is available on our Help page.

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Sunday April 11, 2021 at 03:01

corpname.part: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

descriptivenote.p: Includes materials in English, French, and Italian.

did.abstract: The collection chiefly includes correspondence with writers and other collaborators. The collection also contains project files which include manuscript drafts, book mock ups, and printed materials. The records provide insight into the collaborative process between publisher and writer including correspondence, manuscript drafts, and book proofs.

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: Elysium Press was created in 1980. The press prints and publishes letterpress books, focusing on the works of gay authors, especially those who had been overlooked in the 20th century. The press's books are hand printed and hand bound.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The collection chiefly includes correspondence with writers and other collaborators including Donald Windham, Charles Henri Ford, Ned Rorem, Paul Cadmus, and James Lord . The correspondence was primarily addressed to David Deiss. The collection also contains project files which include manuscript drafts, book mock ups, and printed materials. The records provide insight into the collaborative process between publisher and writer including correspondence, manuscript drafts, and book proofs.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized into two series: Series I: Correspondence, 1983-2015 and Series II. Project files, 1989-2010.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: The materials are open for research. Box 9: Restricted fragile material.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Purchased from Elyisum Books on the Edwin J. Beinecke Fund, 2019.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: The Elysium Press Records is the physical property of the Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, Yale University. Literary rights, including copyright, belong to the authors or their legal heirs and assigns. For further information, consult the appropriate curator.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: The collection was processed adhering to the Press's original filing system.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Most of the correspondence is addressed to David Deiss.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Within the series, the materials are arranged in alphabetical order.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes correspondence from Paul Auster, Kenneth Anger, and Edward Albee.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes correspondence from Charles Henri Ford.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Within the series, the materials are arranged in alphabetical order.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Restricted fragile material. Access copies of digital files may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.
